content.assessment: This proposal directly targets the institutional structure identified as the core of the lens evidence: the three-track parallel system introduced in the early 1990s as an add-on to the prior single-structure system (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01). Converting 6/8-year entry points into a unified comprehensive phase would eliminate the age-10/12 selection point where the centralized exam (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02) currently operates, and would require confronting the statutory free-choice guarantee that the current minister has called non-negotiable (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03). This is the only proposal that would require legislative-level change to the Act on Public Education itself, representing the highest institutional-fit friction but also the most direct engagement with the documented segregation outcomes (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06).
opportunities: The minister's own acknowledgment that declining demographics make the selective system 'less justifiable' and her placement of entrance exams on the review agenda (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10) creates a rare institutional opening for structural discussion, though she has explicitly ruled out abolition intent

Precedent of discretionary denial on segregation grounds (F-live-hungarian_education_system-09) shows the ministry already recognizes and acts on the sorting mechanism this proposal seeks to eliminate structurally
strengths: Directly addresses the structural origin of tracking documented as a three-decade-old institutional add-on (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01) rather than working around it
Engages the segregation outcome that Hungary has been found to lead the EU on (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06), targeting the mechanism rather than its downstream effects
threats: The minister's explicit statement that she is not initiating abolition, only review (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10), signals that full structural delay exceeds the currently stated scope of institutional appetite

Feeder-primary creaming dynamics documented in Encs (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07) suggest that even comprehensive restructuring at the gimnázium entry point may not fully eliminate sorting if primary-level composition effects persist independently
weaknesses: Requires confronting the statutory free-choice guarantee explicitly declared non-negotiable by the sitting minister (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03), a governance obstacle none of the other proposals face as directly

Would need to dismantle or fundamentally repurpose the centralized admission exam infrastructure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02) that currently administers a functioning, if selection-reinforcing, nationwide process
